plant_disease.name: apple apple scab
plant_disease.symptoms: Olive-green to dark brown velvety spots on leaves. Leaves may curl, become distorted and fall prematurely. Fruit develops dark scabby lesions.
plant_disease.management: Apply fungicides such as captan or myclobutanil during spring. Remove fallen leaves to reduce overwintering spores. Plant resistant apple varieties.

plant_disease.name: apple black rot
plant_disease.symptoms: Brown circular lesions with concentric rings on fruit. Leaves develop purple-bordered brown spots. Cankers appear on branches.
plant_disease.management: Prune and destroy infected branches and mummified fruit. Apply fungicides like captan or thiophanate-methyl. Maintain good tree hygiene.

plant_disease.name: apple cedar apple rust
plant_disease.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces. Small tubes with fringed edges appear on leaf undersides. Fruit may develop similar spots.
plant_disease.management: Remove nearby cedar and juniper trees if possible. Apply fungicides containing myclobutanil in spring. Plant resistant apple varieties.

plant_disease.name: apple healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue regular care including proper watering, fertilization, and monitoring for pests.

plant_disease.name: blueberry healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Maintain acidic soil pH (4.5-5.5), proper mulching, and regular watering.

plant_disease.name: cherry including sour powdery mildew
plant_disease.symptoms: White powdery fungal growth on leaves, shoots, and sometimes fruit. Leaves may curl and become distorted. Stunted new growth.
plant_disease.management: Apply sulfur-based or potassium bicarbonate fungicides. Improve air circulation by pruning. Avoid overhead watering.

plant_disease.name: cherry including sour healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper pruning, watering, and fertilization practices.

plant_disease.name: corn maize cercospora leaf spot gray leaf spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Rectangular gray to tan lesions running parallel to leaf veins. Lesions may merge causing large areas of dead leaf tissue.
plant_disease.management: Rotate crops to reduce inoculum. Plant resistant hybrids. Apply foliar fungicides if needed during tasseling. Tillage to bury crop residue.

plant_disease.name: corn maize common rust
plant_disease.symptoms: Small reddish-brown to cinnamon-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces. Pustules may appear on any above-ground plant part.
plant_disease.management: Plant resistant hybrids. Apply foliar fungicides such as azoxystrobin if infection occurs early. Most hybrids have adequate resistance.

plant_disease.name: corn maize northern leaf blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Long elliptical grayish-green to tan lesions (1-6 inches) on leaves. Lesions may start near lower leaves and move upward.
plant_disease.management: Plant resistant hybrids. Rotate crops and use tillage to bury residue. Apply fungicides at early tassel if needed.

plant_disease.name: corn maize healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper fertilization, irrigation, and pest monitoring.

plant_disease.name: grape black rot
plant_disease.symptoms: Circular reddish-brown leaf spots. Fruit develops brown shriveled 'mummies'. Dark pycnidia visible on infected fruit or leaves.
plant_disease.management: Remove mummified fruit and infected plant parts. Apply fungicides (captan, myclobutanil) before bloom. Maintain good vine canopy management.

plant_disease.name: grape esca black measles
plant_disease.symptoms: Interveinal striping and discoloration on leaves. Dark streaking of wood. Fruit may show purple-brown spots. Sudden vine collapse in severe cases.
plant_disease.management: No chemical cure available. Prune out infected wood. Some trunk renewal strategies may help. Avoid large pruning wounds.

plant_disease.name: grape leaf blight isariopsis leaf spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Dark brown spots with yellow halos on leaves. Spots may merge leading to leaf drying and defoliation.
plant_disease.management: Apply fungicides such as mancozeb or copper-based products. Improve air circulation. Remove and destroy infected leaves.

plant_disease.name: grape healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper vine training, pruning, and disease monitoring.

plant_disease.name: orange haunglongbing citrus greening
plant_disease.symptoms: Blotchy mottling and yellowing of leaves. Lopsided, small, and bitter fruit with aborted seeds. Twig dieback.
plant_disease.management: No cure exists. Remove infected trees to prevent spread. Control Asian citrus psyllid vector with insecticides. Plant disease-free nursery stock.

plant_disease.name: peach bacterial spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Small dark water-soaked spots on leaves that may fall out creating a 'shot-hole' appearance. Fruit develops sunken brown lesions.
plant_disease.management: Apply copper-based bactericides during dormancy. Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation.

plant_disease.name: peach healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper pruning, fertilization, and pest management.

plant_disease.name: pepper bell bacterial spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Small dark raised spots on leaves and fruit. Leaves may turn yellow and drop. Fruit spots are raised and scab-like.
plant_disease.management: Use certified disease-free seeds and transplants. Apply copper-based sprays. Rotate crops and avoid overhead irrigation.

plant_disease.name: pepper bell healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper watering, fertilization, and pest monitoring.

plant_disease.name: potato early blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Dark brown to black concentric ring spots (target-like) on older lower leaves. Lesions may cause defoliation. Tubers may develop dark sunken spots.
plant_disease.management: Apply fungicides like chlorothalonil or mancozeb. Rotate crops. Remove volunteer potato plants. Maintain proper nutrition.

plant_disease.name: potato late blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Dark water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems, often starting at leaf tips. White fuzzy mold may appear on undersides. Rapid plant death in wet conditions.
plant_disease.management: Apply fungicides preventatively (chlorothalonil, mancozeb). Destroy infected plants immediately. Avoid overhead irrigation. Plant resistant varieties.

plant_disease.name: potato healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper hilling, watering, and pest monitoring.

plant_disease.name: raspberry healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper cane pruning, mulching, and pest monitoring.

plant_disease.name: soybean healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper crop rotation, fertilization, and weed management.

plant_disease.name: squash powdery mildew
plant_disease.symptoms: White powdery spots on upper leaf surfaces that spread to cover entire leaves. Leaves may yellow and die. Reduced fruit quality.
plant_disease.management: Apply fungicides like sulfur or potassium bicarbonate. Plant resistant varieties. Space plants for good air circulation.

plant_disease.name: strawberry leaf scorch
plant_disease.symptoms: Irregular dark purple blotches on leaves that may merge. Leaf margins appear scorched. Severely affected leaves dry up.
plant_disease.management: Remove infected leaves. Apply fungicides when new growth begins. Renovate beds after harvest. Plant resistant varieties.

plant_disease.name: strawberry healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper mulching, watering, and runner management.

plant_disease.name: tomato bacterial spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Small dark water-soaked spots on leaves, stems, and fruit. Spots may have yellow halos. Severe infections cause defoliation.
plant_disease.management: Use disease-free seeds and transplants. Apply copper-based bactericides. Rotate crops. Avoid working with wet plants.

plant_disease.name: tomato early blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Dark concentric ring spots (target pattern) on lower older leaves. Leaves may yellow and drop. Dark leathery spots on fruit.
plant_disease.management: Apply fungicides (chlorothalonil, mancozeb). Remove lower infected leaves. Stake plants for air circulation. Mulch to prevent soil splash.

plant_disease.name: tomato late blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Large dark water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems. White mold may form on leaf undersides. Rapid plant death in cool, wet weather.
plant_disease.management: Apply protective fungicides. Destroy infected plants immediately. Avoid overhead watering. Plant resistant varieties.

plant_disease.name: tomato leaf mold
plant_disease.symptoms: Pale green to yellowish spots on upper leaf surfaces. Olive-green to grayish-purple fuzzy mold on leaf undersides.
plant_disease.management: Improve greenhouse ventilation. Reduce humidity. Apply fungicides if needed. Plant resistant varieties. Remove infected leaves.

plant_disease.name: tomato septoria leaf spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Numerous small circular spots with dark borders and gray centers on lower leaves. Tiny dark dots (pycnidia) visible in spots.
plant_disease.management: Remove infected lower leaves. Apply fungicides (chlorothalonil, mancozeb). Mulch around plants. Avoid overhead watering.

plant_disease.name: tomato spider mites two spotted spider mite
plant_disease.symptoms: Tiny yellow speckles on leaves (stippling). Fine webbing on leaf undersides. Leaves may bronze and dry out. Tiny mites visible with magnification.
plant_disease.management: Spray plants with strong jet of water to dislodge mites. Apply miticides or insecticidal soap. Introduce predatory mites. Avoid dusty conditions.

plant_disease.name: tomato target spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings on leaves, stems, and fruit. Spots start small and enlarge. Lower leaves affected first.
plant_disease.management: Apply fungicides (chlorothalonil, azoxystrobin). Improve air circulation. Remove infected plant debris. Practice crop rotation.

plant_disease.name: tomato yellow leaf curl virus
plant_disease.symptoms: Severe upward curling and yellowing of leaves. Stunted plant growth. Reduced fruit production. Plants may appear bushy.
plant_disease.management: Control whitefly vectors with insecticides or reflective mulch. Use virus-resistant tomato varieties. Remove infected plants promptly.

plant_disease.name: tomato mosaic virus
plant_disease.symptoms: Light and dark green mottled pattern on leaves. Leaves may be distorted or fern-like. Fruit may show uneven ripening.
plant_disease.management: No chemical cure. Use virus-free seeds and transplants. Disinfect tools and hands. Remove infected plants. Avoid tobacco products near plants.

plant_disease.name: tomato healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. The plant appears healthy.
plant_disease.management: Continue proper staking, watering, fertilization, and pest monitoring.
